entries.text: For location questions asking county/country/state/region, the final answer must match that exact geographic level. If a hop returns a city/town for a county question, ask a follow-up: "Which county is <city/town> in?".

For questions about awards, nominations, or what someone won for a work, include the work/context in the follow-up query and return the full award/category when evidence supports it, not only the work title.

For parent/child/spouse relation questions, preserve the requested relation and reject mixed spans containing both parent and child. Ask "Who is the child of <person>?" or "Who is the mother of <person>?" directly.

For "performer of <song>" or "author of <work>" questions, resolve the performer/author first, then ask the target relation with both the resolved entity and the original work title to avoid broad facts about the person.
For date questions, preserve the event context. If a hop returns an episode/title/place rather than a date, ask a new hop for the date of that bridge clue.
Do not answer Unknown or declare false premise. Return the best supported concise span from evidence, even if support is imperfect.
For acronym answers, preserve the expansion when the question asks what it stands for; for exact names, prefer full canonical names when available in evidence.
For 3-hop or 4-hop questions, do not stop after two hops unless the final target in the original question is fully answered. Check each bridge phrase has been resolved.
